item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Surveillance Gaps, Remuneration Reforms, Crypto Regime
item.description:
Good morning. Let's get into today's compliance briefing, starting with the most operationally significant development for wholesale markets teams.
The 2026 edition of eflow's Global Trends in Market Abuse and Trade Surveillance report is now live, drawing on enforcement data and input from over 300 regulatory professionals. The headline numbers demand attention. Fifty percent of all fines issued globally were related to trade surveillance system and controls failures. That's not insider dealin...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Reform and Regulatory Pipeline Priorities
item.description:
Good morning. The most consequential development for compliance teams right now is the coordinated SMCR reform push from HM Treasury, the FCA, and the PRA, all three publishing consultation papers on 15 July 2025. The consultation window closes 7 October 2025, and if you haven't already started your firm's response, now is the time.
Let's start with what matters most for senior managers and certification. The FCA and PRA are proposing what they're calling Phase 1 reforms — changes that can be m...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Handbook Upgrades and Regulatory Housekeeping
item.description:
Good morning. Today's compliance briefing is relatively light on headline enforcement or market abuse developments, but there are a few housekeeping and strategic items worth noting.
First, the FCA has formally launched its redesigned Handbook website. This is part of the regulator's stated 5-year strategy to become a smarter regulator. The new site improves navigation, makes it easier to understand connections between rules, and critically, allows users to compare different versions of Handboo...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Reform and Enforcement Escalation
item.description: Let's start with the biggest structural development. The UK government is undertaking the most sweeping review of the Senior Managers and Certification Regime since its introduction. This is playing out in three phases, and compliance teams need to understand all of them. Phase One, driven by the FCA and PRA through consultation papers CP25/21 and CP18/25, is essentially a streamlining exercise. The 12-week temporary cover rule is being revised so that firms must submit an SMF application within...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - UK Reporting Overhaul and SMCR Reform
item.description:
Let's get into the key developments that matter for compliance teams across wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets divisions.
The biggest news this month is the FCA's launch of CP25/32, a consultation proposing a fundamental overhaul of the UK's onshore MiFIR transaction reporting regime. If implemented, the changes would reduce the number of reportable fields, limit mandatory reporting to instruments traded on UK venues, remove FX derivatives from scope entirely, and shorten the defaul...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - UK MAR Compliance Framework Essentials
item.description: Let's start with what matters most for trading desks and compliance teams today. The FCA continues to signal that preventing, detecting, and punishing market abuse is among its highest enforcement priorities, directly tied to its statutory objectives of consumer protection, market integrity, and competition. The enforcement toolkit is formidable: unlimited fines for civil breaches of UK MAR, and on the criminal side under the Criminal Justice Act 1993 and Financial Services Act 2012, custodial s...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Enforcement, MAR Actions, Algorithmic Trading
item.description:
Let's get into today's compliance briefing, which covers some significant enforcement actions and a dense set of regulatory developments that wholesale markets teams need to be tracking.
The headline story is the FCA's enforcement momentum in Q1 2026. Total fines have reached just under £16 million for the quarter, and the pattern here is unmistakable — individual accountability is front and centre.
The largest single penalty goes to John Wood Group PLC at £12.9 million for publishing misleadi...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Remuneration Reform, Market Abuse, SMCR Accountability
item.description: Let's start with the most consequential development for wholesale markets compliance teams this period. The FCA and PRA have jointly published their final policy statement on remuneration reform for dual-regulated firms — banks, building societies, and PRA-designated investment firms. These changes took effect on 16 October 2025, and some may be applied optionally to ongoing performance years or unvested awards from prior years. The reforms are significant in scope and go further than the origin...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Market Abuse and SMCR Accountability
item.description:
Good morning. Today's briefing covers two critical areas: the FCA's sharpened stance on market abuse enforcement and the continuing accountability obligations under the Senior Managers and Certification Regime.
Let's start with the big development. On 29 April 2025, Therese Chambers, Joint Executive Director of Enforcement and Market Oversight at the FCA, laid out the regulator's agenda for combatting market abuse as part of its new five-year strategy. She framed enforcement around what she cal...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA PRA Cut SMCR Red Tape
item.description: Good morning. The headline development today is a joint announcement from the FCA and PRA signalling cuts to Senior Managers and Certification Regime red tape, explicitly positioned as a measure to support growth. While the full detail of these changes hasn't yet been published in the materials available, the signal itself is significant. For compliance officers managing SMCR frameworks across wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets divisions, this likely means upcoming revisions to how s...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - UK Regulation Pivots Toward Growth
item.description:
Let's get into today's compliance briefing, which centers on a fundamental shift in the UK's regulatory posture and what it means practically for wholesale markets and investment bank compliance teams.
The headline development comes from EY's 2026 UK Financial Services Regulation Outlook, which maps a regulatory environment that has decisively pivoted from defending against risk to actively enabling economic growth. The government's position is explicit: it is time to regulate for growth. The L...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Geopolitics and Enforcement Landscape Update
item.description:
Good morning. Two developments to flag today, both relatively light on published detail but carrying meaningful implications for compliance teams operating across wholesale markets.
First, enforcement trends around abuse of dominance. A&O Shearman has highlighted that antitrust authorities globally are targeting both exploitative and exclusionary abuses of dominance with increasing vigour. Now, while this sits squarely in the competition law space, there's a compliance read-across worth noting....

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Operational Resilience and SMCR Reform Updates
item.description:
Good morning. The headline development today is a major coordinated move from the FCA, PRA and Bank of England on operational resilience. On 18 March 2026, all three authorities issued Policy Statements finalising their rules on operational incident reporting and material third-party reporting. This is the culmination of consultations that ran through 2024, and the practical implications for wholesale markets compliance teams are significant.
Let's start with what matters most. The regulators h...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - MAR Enforcement and Surveillance Priorities
item.description:
Today's briefing focuses on the FCA's evolving approach to Market Abuse Regulation and what it means practically for compliance teams across wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets divisions.
The headline message from the FCA is a clear shift in expectations: the regulator wants to see market abuse controls that are preventative, not merely detective. It's no longer sufficient to have surveillance systems that flag suspicious activity after the fact. The FCA expects firms to build framew...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Regulatory References and PRA Oversight Basics
item.description:
It's a relatively quiet day on the regulatory front. The sources flagged today are essentially background reference materials rather than new enforcement actions or policy changes, but they touch on themes that remain core to how compliance teams operate.
The PRA continues in its established role as the prudential supervisor for banks, building societies, credit unions, insurers, and major investment firms. For wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets divisions, the PRA's oversight framew...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Enforcement and Resilience Updates
item.description:
Here's your compliance briefing for today, focusing on the latest from the FCA that matters most to wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets teams.
Let's start with enforcement and individual accountability. The FCA has banned Kasim Garipoglu from working in UK financial services. While the full details of the prohibition are still being digested, any ban sends a clear signal about SM&CR enforcement appetite. If you're a compliance officer managing senior manager or certification regime r...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - MAR Enforcement and SMCR Expansion
item.description: Let's start with what matters most this quarter: FCA enforcement on market abuse. The regulator has been busy sending clear signals to trading desks and senior management alike. The most significant action is a fine of nearly thirteen million pounds against John Wood Group for failing to take reasonable care that its announcements were not false or misleading. The FCA's focus here was squarely on the absence of adequate systems, controls, and procedures — the kind of infrastructure that complian...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Non-Financial Misconduct & Regulatory Outlook
item.description:
Good morning. Today's compliance briefing focuses on several developments that wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets teams should have on their radar.
The most immediately actionable item is the FCA's PS25/23 on tackling non-financial misconduct, which comes into effect for non-bank SMCR firms from September 2026. This is a significant expansion of the conduct perimeter under the Senior Managers and Certification Regime. Firms need to start thinking now about how non-financial miscondu...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - MAR Enforcement and Listing Reforms
item.description:
Good morning. Let's get into today's compliance briefing, which covers significant FCA enforcement activity under UK MAR, major structural changes to the listing and prospectus regime, and developments in SMCR accountability.
Starting with what matters most to trading desks and surveillance teams: the FCA has been active on enforcement this quarter, sending clear signals about its expectations. The headline action is a nearly thirteen million pound fine imposed on John Wood Group PLC on the fou...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Insider Dealing Fines and SMCR Reporting Relief
item.description: Let's start with enforcement. The FCA has fined Russell Gerrity just over £309,000 for insider dealing. Gerrity was a consultant working on oil rig decommissioning projects and used confidential information about planned corporate transactions to trade shares ahead of public announcements, making profits of roughly £129,000. This is a textbook inside information case, but what matters for compliance teams is the FCA's methodology: they built the case through detailed analysis of information acce...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Market Abuse Reform and Lending Rules
item.description:
Good morning. Today's briefing covers two significant areas: fundamental changes to the UK's market abuse regime and PRA mortgage lending reforms.
Let's start with what matters most for wholesale markets compliance. HM Treasury and the FCA have completed their joint review of the criminal market abuse regime and the direction of travel is clear — enforcement is getting sharper. Three things stand out. First, the criminal market abuse framework is being expanded to cover a wider range of fixed i...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - UK Product Regulation Framework Update
item.description: Today's item is a brief one, but it's worth flagging. The UK government has published a consultation page on what it's calling the UK's new core product regulation, market surveillance, and enforcement framework. Now, the substantive detail available from the published page is limited — it's essentially a placeholder on GOV.UK signalling that this consultation exists — but the framing matters. Post-Brexit, the UK has been steadily building out its own regulatory architecture, and this consultati...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Accountability and AML Enforcement Intensify
item.description:
Good morning. Two significant enforcement trends are converging right now that demand attention from compliance teams across wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets divisions.
Let's start with the most consequential development: the FCA is escalating its focus on individual senior manager accountability under SMCR, and the recent cases of Kasim Garipoglu and Howard Duckett tell the story clearly. On 13 March 2026, the FCA issued its Final Notice banning Garipoglu from performing any func...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - AI Governance and SM&CR Accountability
item.description:
Good morning. Today's briefing centres on a critically important area for compliance teams: the regulatory framework around AI in financial services, and what it means for accountability under SM&CR.
Let's start with the headline message from the FCA. CEO Nikhil Rathi confirmed in December 2025 that the FCA will not introduce AI-specific rules. His reasoning is straightforward — the technology evolves every three to six months, making prescriptive regulation impractical. Instead, the FCA is rel...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Market Abuse Enforcement Intensifies
item.description: Let's start with the enforcement picture. The FCA has already issued over sixteen million pounds in fines in 2026, and the pattern is unmistakable — market abuse is the dominant theme. The most significant corporate penalty came against John Wood Group PLC, fined nearly thirteen million pounds for breaching Listing Rules through the publication of misleading information. But for compliance teams at investment banks, the action to watch closely is Dinosaur Merchant Bank, fined three hundred and t...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SM&CR Reform and Regulatory Grid Update
item.description: The most significant development for compliance teams today is the FCA's publication of Consultation Paper CP25/21, which proposes substantial reforms to the Senior Managers and Certification Regime. This is a two-phase effort. Phase one targets immediate practical improvements — greater flexibility around the 12-week rule for temporary senior management function cover, extended validity periods for criminal record checks in SMF applications, streamlined certification processes to reduce duplica...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - UK MAR Post-Brexit Surveillance Expectations
item.description:
Good morning. Today's briefing covers the UK market abuse regulation landscape following Brexit and what it means practically for surveillance and compliance operations at investment banks.
The headline point is straightforward but operationally significant: UK MAR now stands as an independent regime, separate from EU MAR. For Global Banking and Markets divisions operating across jurisdictions, this means dual compliance obligations are a reality, not a theoretical concern. Firms must ensure th...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - PRA FCA Remuneration Rules Overhaul
item.description:
Good morning. Today's briefing covers a major overhaul of UK banker remuneration rules that compliance teams across wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets divisions need to act on immediately.
The PRA and FCA have announced sweeping reforms to senior banker pay rules, effective October 16, 2025. These changes apply to 2025 pay awards and any outstanding payments, so the implementation window is essentially now.
The headline change: bonus deferral periods for senior bankers are being ha...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - MAR Reforms, Enforcement Trends, Sanctions Escalation
item.description:
Good morning. Several significant developments are converging that demand attention from compliance teams across wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets divisions.
Let's start with market abuse. ESMA published a consultation on 19 February proposing amendments to its MAR guidelines on delayed disclosure of inside information. This aligns with the Listing Act changes coming into force on 5 June 2026. The key shift: issuers will no longer need to immediately disclose inside information rel...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA PRA Joint Enforcement Action
item.description: Today's briefing is necessarily brief given limited source material, but there's a point worth flagging. The FCA and PRA jointly fined R. Raphael & Sons plc — a case that, while dating back to 2019, serves as a useful reminder of how the dual-regulator model operates in practice when it comes to enforcement. Joint fines from both the FCA and PRA signal that where a firm falls under both supervisory regimes, neither regulator is content to defer to the other. Both will pursue action independently...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Banker Bonus Rules Relaxed Immediately
item.description:
Good morning. Today's key development for compliance teams at UK investment banks concerns a significant loosening of the remuneration rules for Material Risk Takers at dual-regulated firms. On 16 October 2025, the FCA and PRA published a joint policy statement that went further than their original consultation proposals, and the changes took effect immediately.
Let's get into the substance. The most impactful change is to deferral periods. Previously, Senior Management Function holders faced d...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - UK Enforcement Decline Hits Financial Regulation
item.description:
Today's briefing focuses on a significant and broad-based decline in UK regulatory enforcement in 2025, with direct implications for compliance teams across wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets divisions.
Let's start with the headline numbers from the Financial Conduct Authority. FCA penalties dropped to £196 million in 2025, down from approximately £800 million the prior year — a fall of nearly £600 million. Half as many firms were subject to enforcement action compared to 2024. Now,...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Enforcement Trends and Market Abuse Evolution
item.description:
Good morning. Today's briefing covers enforcement trends that UK compliance teams need to track closely, alongside market conditions that are raising the stakes on surveillance and controls.
Let's start with the most consequential theme: individual accountability is now the defining feature of regulatory enforcement on both sides of the Atlantic. The SEC has made explicitly clear heading into 2026 that corporate penalties alone will not suffice — they are pursuing personal liability against CEO...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Reforms Cut Compliance Burden
item.description:
The headline development today is the FCA and PRA jointly confirming concrete changes to the Senior Managers and Certification Regime, marking the conclusion of the Phase 1 reforms that were first proposed in July 2025. The regulators are framing this as a meaningful step toward halving the overall regulatory burden SMCR places on firms, and for compliance teams at wholesale banks this has immediate practical implications.
Let's start with what matters most for day-to-day operations. Firms now ...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SM&CR Phase 1 Reforms Take Effect
item.description: The most significant development for compliance teams this week is the finalisation of SM&CR Phase 1 reforms. On 22 April 2026, the FCA published PS26/6 and the PRA published PS12/26, setting out changes that largely took effect on 24 April, with system and reporting process updates following on 10 July. Alongside these, HM Treasury published its consultation response confirming the legislative direction for Phase 2, which will require primary legislation to be introduced when parliamentary time...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA SM&CR Reforms Take Effect
item.description:
Today's briefing centres on a significant development for anyone working under the Senior Managers and Certification Regime. On 22 April 2026, the FCA published policy statement PS26/6, confirming a package of SM&CR reforms that took effect almost immediately on 24 April. This is Phase One of a broader, multi-phase effort by the FCA, PRA, and HM Treasury to reduce regulatory burden while preserving the core accountability principles that underpin the regime.
Let's start with what's changed righ...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Global Finfluencer Enforcement Crackdown
item.description:
Good morning. One notable development to flag today from the FCA. On the 24th of April, the FCA announced it had spearheaded a global coordinated enforcement action targeting illegal financial influencers — so-called finfluencers. Seventeen regulators worldwide participated in what was dubbed a 'week of action' beginning the 20th of April 2026, combining enforcement activity, consumer awareness campaigns, and educational programmes aimed at influencers who want to operate within the rules.
In t...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Market Sounding Review Findings
item.description:
Good morning. The most significant development for compliance teams today is the FCA's publication of its multi-firm review into market sounding practices across UK equity capital markets. This is a substantive piece of supervisory work and carries direct implications for anyone overseeing ECM activity, syndicate desks, or inside information controls.
The FCA examined data from five banks covering UK equity and equity-linked transactions over fifty million pounds — ninety percent of which were ...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Phase 1 Reforms Take Effect
item.description:
Good morning. Today's briefing focuses on the most significant development for compliance teams in some time: the confirmed first wave of reforms to the Senior Managers and Certification Regime, which largely took effect on the 24th of April 2026.
Let's start with the big picture. The FCA, PRA, and HM Treasury have all published their final positions following the July 2025 consultations that came out of the Leeds Reforms. The regulators received strong support and have implemented proposals la...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Streamlining Reforms Consultation Launched
item.description:
Good morning. Today's briefing centres on a significant development for anyone operating under the Senior Managers and Certification Regime. The FCA and PRA have jointly published consultation papers proposing the first phase of SMCR reforms, with responses due by the 7th of October 2025. This sits alongside a separate government consultation on legislative changes that could prove even more consequential.
Let's start with what matters most. The headline reform is the fix to the so-called 12-we...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Phase 1 Reforms and Phase 2 Preview
item.description:
The FCA's Policy Statement PS26/6 has now delivered the first wave of SMCR reforms, and if you're running compliance for a wholesale markets desk or a Global Banking and Markets division, there are immediate practical implications alongside some significant structural changes on the horizon.
Let's start with what's already live. From 24 April 2026, the 12-week rule for senior management appointments has changed materially. Firms now have 12 weeks to submit an application rather than needing app...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Phase 1 Reforms Take Effect
item.description:
The FCA and PRA published their Phase 1 SMCR reforms on 21 April, with most changes taking effect from 24 April 2026. While the reforms are broadly welcomed as reducing administrative burden and affording firms more flexibility, there are some provisions that demand very careful attention from compliance teams and senior managers alike.
Let me start with what I think is the most consequential change: the new guidance at COCON 4.2.16G on Senior Conduct Rule 2. The FCA has now made explicit what ...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Priorities, AR Reform, SM&CR Expansion
item.description:
Good morning. Let's get into today's compliance briefing, which centres on several significant FCA and PRA developments that wholesale markets teams and compliance officers need to track carefully.
The most consequential development for SM&CR accountability is the Treasury's consultation on reforming the Appointed Representatives regime, published 12 February and closing 9 April 2026. The government is proposing to bring ARs within the scope of the Senior Managers and Certification Regime. Curr...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - MAR Closed Period Notifications and Holiday Liquidity
item.description: A relatively quiet day on the regulatory front, but a few items worth flagging for wholesale markets compliance teams. First, the FCA has published its updated Regulatory Initiatives Grid. Details are sparse in the announcement itself, but this is your signal to pull down the latest version and cross-reference it against your forward-looking compliance programme. Any shifts in timelines for wholesale markets initiatives — particularly around market abuse surveillance expectations or post-trade t...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SM&CR Phase 1 Reforms Now Live
item.description:
Good morning. The headline for compliance teams today is that the FCA's SM&CR Phase 1 reforms are now live following publication of PS26/6 on 22 April. Let me walk through what this means in practice, particularly for wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets divisions.
First, the critical framing: this is not a deregulatory moment. The FCA has stripped out repetitive administrative requirements, but the underlying accountability expectations are unchanged or arguably sharpened. The regula...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - CMA Reform and Competition Policy Shifts
item.description: Today's focus is on the significant reshaping of UK competition enforcement and what it means for compliance teams in wholesale markets and banking. The CMA underwent dramatic change through 2025, beginning with the unexpected removal of chair Marcus Bokkerink and his replacement by former Amazon executive Doug Gurr, driven by the government's conclusion that CMA leadership wasn't sufficiently aligned with its economic growth agenda. The CMA then implemented its '4Ps' framework — pace, proportio...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Enforcement and Market Abuse Penalties
item.description: Today's key development concerns the FCA's January 2026 enforcement actions, which totaled approximately £371,700 in fines against two individuals for market misconduct. Richard Adam received the larger penalty at £232,800, with the FCA's Final Notice citing his knowing involvement in breaches of Article 15 of the Market Abuse Regulation — the prohibition on market manipulation — alongside infractions of Listing Rule 1.3.3R, Listing Principle 1, and Premium Listing Principle 2. Zafar Khan was fi...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Market Abuse Fundamentals and Identification
item.description: Today's note is a brief reminder on fundamentals. Market abuse, as defined under UK MAR, occurs when individuals exploit unethical practices that undermine market integrity and put other participants at a disadvantage. The critical point for compliance teams in wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets divisions is that no asset class is immune — shares, commodities, and forex are all explicitly within scope. This reinforces the expectation that trade surveillance systems and controls canno...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Phase 1 Reform: Process Not Substance
item.description:
Today's briefing centres on the FCA's SMCR Phase 1 reforms, which are now confirmed through Policy Statements PS24/3 and PS25/1, with implementation staggered across 2025 and 2026. The critical message for compliance teams at wholesale banks and Global Banking & Markets divisions is this: these reforms are process improvements, not governance relaxations. The FCA's enforcement posture on individual accountability has not softened.
Let me walk through what actually changes. The most operationall...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Market Abuse Surveillance Gaps Widen
item.description:
Today's briefing centres on a stark finding from eflow Global's survey of 300 senior finance executives across the UK, US, Europe, and APAC: while 63% of regulatory leaders feel confident keeping up with general regulatory change, nearly half identify market abuse regulations as a significant compliance challenge. For UK-based firms, this figure jumps to 65%, making it the most pressing jurisdiction-specific concern.
The backdrop here is critical. Market abuse-related enforcement actions have i...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Remuneration Reform and SMCR Obligations
item.description: The most significant development today concerns the PRA and FCA jointly publishing Policy Statement PS25/15 on remuneration reform, with changes set to take effect on 15 October 2025. This follows the consultation paper CP23/24 which closed in March this year, so we're now looking at confirmed final rules. These changes apply to banks, building societies, and PRA-designated investment firms, which means this lands squarely on the desks of wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets compliance...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Reforms and FCA Enforcement Updates
item.description:
Let's start with enforcement. The FCA has fined Dinosaur Merchant Bank Limited three hundred and thirty-eight thousand pounds. Details on the specific breach remain limited at this stage, but the action is a clear reminder that the FCA's enforcement apparatus continues to reach smaller firms in the merchant banking space. Compliance teams across wholesale markets should take note—no firm is too small to attract regulatory scrutiny.
Now to the most operationally significant development this peri...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Reform and MAR Developments
item.description:
Good morning. Today's compliance briefing touches on two developments worth keeping on your radar, though we should note that full detail on both was limited in the source material available.
First, on the Senior Managers and Certification Regime — SMCR reform is anticipated in 2026, and Kingsley Napley has flagged this as a significant upcoming shift. While the granular details of the proposed changes weren't fully available in today's source, the signal is clear: compliance officers and senio...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Phase 1 Reform Implementation Guide
item.description:
Here's your SMCR compliance briefing for today. The big story is the FCA, PRA, and Treasury's joint publication of the SMCR Phase 1 reforms under PS26/6, and what they mean practically for firms preparing to implement changes across three staggered effective dates this year.
Let's start with what matters most. The FCA's stated goal is increasing the efficiency and effectiveness of SMCR while reducing unnecessary regulatory burden. Phase 1 is characterised as a procedural clean-up — helpful but ...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - MAR Compliance Obligations and Enforcement
item.description:
Good morning. Today's briefing focuses on the UK Market Abuse Regulation framework and its practical compliance implications, which remain central to every wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets operation.
Let's start with what matters most: enforcement exposure. UK MAR, which was on-shored from EU legislation on 31 December 2020 via the European Union Withdrawal Act, gives the FCA sweeping investigative and enforcement powers. The penalties for breach are severe and deliberately design...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - King's Speech Reshapes SMCR and Regulation
item.description:
Today's briefing centres on the King's Speech delivered on 13 May 2026, which sets out the government's legislative programme — though it comes under considerable political uncertainty, with leadership speculation still gripping the Labour Party. For compliance teams in wholesale markets and investment banking, there are several significant developments to track.
The headline item is the Enhancing Financial Services Bill, which confirms that SMCR Phase 2 reform is moving from regulatory adjustm...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Market Abuse Surveillance and Financial Crime Enforcement
item.description:
Good morning. Here's your compliance briefing for today, focusing on enforcement actions, surveillance expectations, and what this means practically for wholesale markets and Global Banking and Markets teams.
The most significant development to flag is a recent FCA final notice targeting multiple failures in a firm's market abuse surveillance arrangements. This is a clear signal that the FCA is actively testing whether firms' surveillance infrastructure is genuinely fit for purpose — not just w...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Regulatory Initiatives Grid Key Developments
item.description:
The Financial Services Regulatory Initiatives Forum has published its eighth Regulatory Initiatives Grid, released on 19 May 2026, and there are several developments here that wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets compliance teams need to pay close attention to.
Let me start with what matters most for enforcement and individual accountability. The FCA is consulting on changes to its Decision Procedure and Penalties Manual — DEPP. This is significant. The proposals include an increase t...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SM&CR Phase 1 Reforms Announced
item.description: Good morning. Today's briefing centres on the most consequential development for SM&CR compliance in some time. On the 22nd of April 2026, the FCA and PRA jointly announced the first phase of reforms to the Senior Managers and Certification Regime. We're now seeing the first detailed analyses from major law firms, with both Fox Williams and Sidley publishing guidance in the last couple of days — Fox Williams on the 18th of May and Sidley on the 19th — which tells you the market is taking this se...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Regulatory Structure and Data Enforcement Trends
item.description:
Good morning. Today's briefing covers two areas relevant to compliance teams: a reminder of the UK's regulatory architecture and its implications for supervised firms, and the rapidly escalating GDPR enforcement landscape that affects data handling across wholesale markets operations.
Starting with the more immediately actionable topic: GDPR enforcement has reached a new intensity. European regulators issued €1.2 billion in penalties in 2025 alone, and breach notifications surged 22% year-over-...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - No Relevant Compliance Updates Today
item.description: This is your daily compliance briefing. Unfortunately, today's source materials did not contain any substantive content relevant to UK investment bank compliance teams. The feeds tagged under UK Market Abuse Regulation and SMCR Compliance Updates returned only a retail investment platform advertisement and unrelated video content — neither of which contained any regulatory developments, enforcement actions, surveillance guidance, or Senior Managers and Certification Regime updates. There are no ...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - PRA Prudential Framework Recalibration Roadmap
item.description:
Good morning. Today's briefing is dominated by a significant speech from PRA Executive Director David Bailey, delivered on 28 April, laying out the most comprehensive roadmap we've seen for reforming the UK banking prudential framework. This matters enormously for wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets divisions, so let's unpack what's most relevant.
The headline number is this: the Financial Policy Committee has reduced its benchmark for Tier 1 capital requirements from 14% to 13% of r...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Operational Incident Reporting Rules
item.description:
Good morning. Today's key development for compliance teams concerns the FCA's forthcoming operational incident reporting rules and what they mean for enhanced SMCR firms. TLT has published guidance flagging the essential points firms need to be across.
The FCA is formalising expectations around how firms report material operational incidents — and this has direct implications for senior manager accountability. Under the SMCR framework, the individuals holding prescribed responsibilities for ope...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Operational Incident Reporting Rules
item.description:
Today's key development for compliance teams concerns the FCA's new operational incident reporting rules and what they mean in practice for firms operating under the core or limited SMCR framework. TLT has flagged the essentials, and the message is clear: firms need to act now rather than wait for implementation deadlines to approach.
The FCA is broadening the scope of mandatory incident reporting, which means a wider range of operational disruptions — including technology failures and third-pa...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Algorithmic Collusion and Market Abuse Gaps
item.description:
Today's briefing centres on a significant new academic paper published in ERA Forum by Ian Gauci of GTG Legal, examining what the author calls 'unilateral parallel algorithmic behaviour' in EU financial services law — and its direct relevance to how UK regulators and compliance teams should be thinking about algorithmic trading risk.
The core argument is this: competing firms deploying independent pricing algorithms can converge on supra-competitive prices without any agreement, communication, ...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Regulatory Updates: UK and US Developments
item.description: Today's briefing is a lighter one, but there are a couple of cross-border regulatory developments worth flagging for compliance teams. Starting with the US side, on April 28th the SEC issued an order adjusting the dollar-amount thresholds for 'qualified client' status under Rule 205-3 of the Investment Advisers Act. These inflation-adjusted thresholds take effect in June 2026, so if your firm has US-facing operations or advisory arrangements involving performance fees, now is the time to review ...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - Evidencing Reasonable Steps Under SMCR
item.description:
Good morning. Today's briefing focuses on a critical and perennial theme under the Senior Managers and Certification Regime: how SMF holders can practically evidence that they have taken reasonable steps to prevent or detect regulatory breaches within their areas of responsibility.
The key message from updated guidance published by Lee Werrell at Compliant Business Management is blunt but important — when the FCA comes knocking after a regulatory breach, saying 'I didn't know' will not protect ...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Phase 1 Reforms Take Effect
item.description:
Good morning. Today's compliance briefing focuses almost entirely on the SMCR Phase 1 reforms, which represent the most significant practical changes to individual accountability requirements that compliance teams in wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets divisions have had to implement in some time.
The FCA's PS26/6 and the PRA's PS12/26 are now both live, with the majority of Phase 1 changes effective since 24 April 2026. Two further implementation dates follow — 10 July and 1 Septemb...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Sector Priorities and AI Act Enforcement
item.description: Here's your compliance briefing for today. The most significant development for wholesale markets teams is the FCA's new approach to communicating regulatory expectations. The regulator has formally replaced its portfolio letters with nine annual Regulatory Priorities reports, each tailored to a specific sector. Critically for those of us in Global Banking and Markets, the Wholesale Markets report and the Wholesale Buy Side report were both published on 19 March 2026 and are now live. These are ...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - PRA May 2026 Regulatory Digest
item.description: Good morning. A brief but important note today: the PRA has published its May 2026 Regulatory Digest on the Bank of England website. Unfortunately, the full substantive content of the digest was not available in the material we received — only the page shell and cookie notice came through. That said, the fact that it has been published means compliance teams, particularly those supporting wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets divisions, need to go directly to the Bank of England site an...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - GDPR Enforcement Tracker Update Overview
item.description: Today's briefing touches on a development that sits at the intersection of data protection and financial services compliance — the GDPR Enforcement Tracker, which now records 3,194 enforcement actions across EU and EEA jurisdictions, with total fines reaching €6.31 billion. That is a staggering figure, and the pace is not slowing down. In 2026 alone, 149 new cases have already been logged, and 176 have appeared in just the last six months. Importantly for us, UK GDPR fines are included in this d...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - MAR Listing Act Reforms Take Effect
item.description:
Good morning. Today's compliance briefing focuses on the most significant changes to the Market Abuse Regulation framework in years, driven by the EU Listing Act that completed its phased implementation on the fifth of June 2026.
Let's start with what matters most operationally. The reformed Article 17 of MAR introduces what's being called the two-step approach to inside information disclosure in protracted processes. Under the old regime, intermediate steps in a deal process — think board appr...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Wholesale Priorities and Operational Resilience
item.description:
Good morning. Let's get into the key regulatory developments that matter for compliance teams across wholesale markets and Global Banking and Markets divisions.
The biggest development this fortnight is the FCA's publication of its 2026 regulatory priorities for wholesale and wholesale buy-side markets. This is a significant shift — these new annual priority reports replace the old portfolio letters and signal a more risk-based, outcomes-focused supervisory posture. The themes running through b...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Strategy and SMCR Conduct Rules
item.description:
Good morning. Two items to cover today that are relevant for compliance teams across wholesale markets and broader regulated firms.
First, the more significant development: the FCA has published what it describes as a strict three-year regulatory strategy. While the full detail of the strategy document warrants close reading, the signal here is clear — the FCA is laying down a marker on intensified oversight, and trade surveillance is squarely in the frame. For those of you in Global Banking an...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - SMCR Reform Consultation Underway
item.description: Good morning. Today's key development for compliance teams is the joint consultation launched by the FCA, PRA, and HM Treasury on reforming the Senior Managers and Certification Regime. While the full detail of the consultation document wasn't available in today's source, the fact that all three bodies are coordinating on this signals that we should expect meaningful changes to the SMCR framework rather than minor tweaks. For those of us in wholesale markets and Global Banking & Markets division...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Authorisation and SMCR Readiness
item.description:
Today's briefing touches on the FCA's evolving expectations around authorisation and governance readiness, with several regulatory developments worth flagging for compliance teams.
First, the overarching message from advisory practice is clear: the FCA is scrutinising applications with increasing rigour, and firms seeking authorisation or expanding permissions need to anticipate supervisory concerns well before submission. This isn't just about document compilation — it's about ensuring governa...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Surveillance Standards and MAR Compliance
item.description: Today's focus is squarely on the FCA's sharpening expectations around market abuse surveillance, driven by the publication of MarketWatch 79. The central message from the regulator is a direct challenge to firms: is your surveillance model genuinely fit for purpose, and are your systems actually effective at catching abuse? This isn't rhetorical. The FCA is signalling that it will be testing firms on the specifics — how alert parameters are set, how often calibration is reviewed, and whether sur...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - MAR 2026 Changes: Enforcement and Readiness
item.description:
The most important development for compliance teams today concerns the Listing Act's overhaul of the Market Abuse Regulation, which entered into force on the fifth of June 2026 — the biggest set of MAR changes since the regulation was introduced in 2016. Let me walk you through what matters most for wholesale markets and trading desk compliance.
The headline change is how protracted processes are now handled. MAR now formally distinguishes intermediate steps from the final event in a multi-stag...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - FCA Authorisation and Regulatory Readiness
item.description:
Today's briefing touches on several threads relevant to compliance teams, though the primary content centres on FCA authorisation standards and a handful of emerging regulatory themes worth flagging.
First, on authorisation: the FCA's approach to determining applications remains intensely focused on how firms articulate risk, governance, and credibility. The regulator is looking beyond documentation at face value — it's interpreting business models, stress-testing the alignment of systems and c...

item.title: Compliance Daily Briefing - PRA April 2025 Regulatory Digest
item.description: Today's briefing is a brief one, and I want to be upfront about that. The PRA has published its April 2025 Regulatory Digest, but the substantive content available from the public extract is extremely limited — essentially just the publication notice itself without detailed regulatory updates making it through to our feed. That said, the fact that the PRA continues its monthly digest cadence matters. These digests typically consolidate prudential policy updates, supervisory statements, and consu...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description:
This week in compliance has been dominated by a single, unmistakable theme: the UK regulatory architecture is being fundamentally recalibrated, and at the center of nearly every development sits individual accountability.
The most structurally significant story running through the entire week is the overhaul of the Senior Managers and Certification Regime. Multiple episodes returned to this from different angles, building a remarkably consistent picture. HM Treasury, the FCA, and the PRA are al...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description:
This week in compliance, one overarching theme dominated every conversation: the UK regulatory landscape is being fundamentally reshaped around a growth agenda, but that pivot toward enabling economic activity is being paired with a more sophisticated, more targeted, and in many respects more demanding supervisory apparatus. Compliance teams that hear "growth" and think "relaxation" will find themselves badly exposed.
The most consequential development landed at the start of the week with the F...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description:
This week in compliance has been remarkably dense, and when you step back and look across all the briefings together, a few powerful themes emerge that should be shaping how compliance teams think about priorities right now.
The single loudest signal this week is the FCA's relentless focus on systems, controls, and preventative governance. This came through in almost every briefing, and it's worth pausing on because it represents a genuine philosophical shift. The regulator is no longer content...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description:
This week in compliance, one theme towers above everything else: regulators are no longer simply asking whether controls exist on paper — they are testing whether those controls actually work under real-world conditions. That shift in emphasis runs through every major development this week, from market abuse enforcement to AI governance, from SMCR accountability to AML screening, and it carries immediate practical consequences for compliance teams across wholesale markets and beyond.
The most p...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description:
This week in compliance, a single unmistakable current runs beneath every major development: regulators are simultaneously streamlining the structural mechanics of compliance while sharpening the consequences for those who fail to meet substantive expectations. That tension — between simplification on one hand and intensified enforcement on the other — is the defining theme, and it touches everything from market abuse surveillance to remuneration rules to sanctions.
Let's start with where the p...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description:
This week in compliance, a clear and somewhat paradoxical picture emerges from across the UK regulatory landscape. The overarching theme is one of tension — between a political environment that is openly pushing for lighter-touch regulation in the name of economic growth, and a personal accountability framework under the Senior Managers and Certification Regime that remains fully intact and, arguably, more consequential than ever precisely because of that loosening.
Let's start with the broades...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description:
This week in compliance, the overarching story is one of a regulatory landscape in deliberate transition — a world where the formal apparatus of accountability is being streamlined and lightened even as the underlying expectations for genuine, operational control have never been higher. That tension runs through virtually every development we've covered, and understanding it is essential for anyone sitting in a compliance function at a wholesale bank or financial institution.
The most prominent...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description:
This week in compliance has been dominated by a single, unmistakable theme: the reshaping of individual accountability under the Senior Managers and Certification Regime. Across multiple episodes, we've tracked the SMCR reforms from consultation through to implementation, and the picture that emerges is one of a regime that is simultaneously loosening its administrative grip while sharpening its substantive teeth.
The Phase 1 reforms that took effect on the 24th of April represent the most sign...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description: This week in compliance, one theme dominated above all others: the evolving shape of individual accountability under the Senior Managers and Certification Regime. Two separate episodes addressed the FCA's Phase 1 reforms in considerable depth, and when read together they deliver a message that every compliance officer in wholesale banking needs to internalize. These reforms are real, they are live, and they are emphatically not a relaxation of the accountability framework. They are an administra...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description: This week in compliance, one theme towers above everything else: the Senior Managers and Certification Regime is undergoing its most significant reform since inception, and the message from regulators is unmistakable — the process is getting simpler, but the accountability is not getting softer. Across virtually every episode this week, SMCR reform appeared in some form, making it the dominant thread running through the entire digest. Alongside it, market abuse surveillance emerged as a second m...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description: This week in compliance has been remarkably dense, and what emerges when you step back and look across all the briefings is a single, unifying narrative: the UK regulatory landscape is being fundamentally recalibrated, not relaxed. Every major development this week, from legislative announcements to prudential roadmaps to enforcement actions, carries the same underlying message — less prescriptive process, more substantive accountability, and regulators who are actively testing whether firms can...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description:
This week in compliance, one overarching theme dominates everything else: regulators on both sides of the Atlantic are simultaneously recalibrating their frameworks, and the message to firms is clear — engage now, don't wait for final rules.
The most substantial development came from PRA Executive Director David Bailey's speech laying out what is genuinely the most comprehensive roadmap we've seen for reforming the UK banking prudential framework. The Financial Policy Committee has reduced its ...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description:
This week in compliance, one theme dominates above all others: the architecture of individual accountability in UK financial services is being actively rebuilt, and the transition demands real operational attention right now, even as the destination promises to be simpler.
The centerpiece is the SMCR Phase 1 reforms, which are now live following the FCA's PS26/6 and the PRA's PS12/26. These represent the most consequential set of practical changes to individual accountability in some time, and ...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description:
This week in compliance, three distinct but deeply interconnected stories emerged, and together they paint a clear picture of a regulatory environment that is simultaneously modernising, tightening, and demanding more from firms than ever before.
The overarching theme is accountability — accountability for data, for market conduct, for operational resilience, and for the systems and relationships firms rely on. Whether we're looking at the staggering growth of GDPR enforcement across Europe, th...

item.title: Weekly Digest: Compliance
item.description:
This week in compliance has been dominated by one unmistakable signal: the UK regulatory framework is entering a phase of simultaneous tightening and modernization, and the pace is accelerating. Across every episode this week, whether the topic was operational resilience, market abuse surveillance, or individual accountability, the same underlying message came through — regulators are moving from principles to practice, and they expect firms to do the same.
The single biggest theme running thro...
